title: Light-night palette coherence
oneline: Hue-flip scorer ranks catalog; punched-light-conditioned night regen fixes ant blanket, all gates pass
summary: An offline region-hue scorer (pen-segmented regions, chroma-weighted circular mean hue, cool-shift credit) correctly flags the ant blanket and ranks all 96 cells; worst offenders are spider-tall (98%), the shapes wides (~90%), and farm/cat-tall (80%). Conditioning the night generation on the light fill as a second image works, but ONLY with the shipped punched (line-art-free) fill as the reference: the raw's black outlines made the model re-ink dark lines in 7/7 takes despite explicit prompting. The punched-reference take passed all four standard gates first try and cut ant-wide's hue-flip area from 61.1% to 17.3% (red blanket preserved at night).
tried: Region-hue flip scorer: pen-outline segmentation, background flood-excluded, chroma-weighted circular mean hue per region, >75deg flip threshold with 25deg toward-blue moonlight credit
Full-catalog ranking (96 cells) + visual spot-checks of spider-tall, teddy-tall, duck-wide
Night regen conditioned on the light RAW as second image (3 calls, t0.6 ladder)
Same with explicit anti-black-line prompt, t0.3, --dilate-lines 2 (4 calls)
Night regen conditioned on the shipped PUNCHED light fill (1 call)
worked: Scorer flags ant-wide's blanket (red->cyan, 12k px) and matches eyeballs on every spot-check
Punched-light conditioning: all 4 gates pass first try (drift 0.0000, bgLuma 72, lineW 173, eyes ok), hue flips 61.1% -> 17.3%
Scoring the conditioned take with the same scorer closed the loop (raw-ref takes measured 0.0% flip)
failed: Conditioning on the light RAW: the model copies its BLACK outlines (lineW 69-75, unshippable) in 7/7 takes — prompt wording, low temp, and dilated lines all failed to stop it
limitations: 8-call cap: only ant-wide regenerated; teddy-tall/duck-wide untested end-to-end
Conditioned takes trend brighter (bgLuma 48-72 vs typical 15-50) — 'dimmed day' pulls against deep-night mood
Residual 17.3%: left flowers went dusty mauve in the single kept take (no best-of-N budget)
Scorer flags some legitimate night changes (warm glowing windows) and thresholds are uncalibrated

comparisons.label: nature/ant-wide light vs night
comparisons.note: Shipped night flips the red/white blanket to navy/green and the yellow flowers to purple; the conditioned take keeps the day palette (see before-light.webp), dimmed for night, and passes all gates

images.caption: Shipped light raw: red/white blanket, yellow flowers
Shipped night raw: blanket navy/green, flowers purple — the hue flip
Conditioned night take (punched-light reference): palette-coherent, all gates pass
Raw-reference failure mode: perfect palette (0% flip) but black outlines copied from the reference
